item.title: The Shocking Case of Amy Carlson
item.description: The Shocking Case of Amy Carlson

media:content.media:title: The Shocking Case of Amy Carlson
media:content.media:description: The Shocking Case of Amy Carlson

item.title: Ezra McCandless: The Revenge of Killer Ex-Girlfriend
item.description:
On the afternoon of March 22, 2018, Ezra McCandless stabbed 24-year-old philosophy student Alex Woodworth 16 times in a dairy farm near Eau Claire, Wisconsin.
Four hours after he was stabbed, Ezra knocked on the door of a dairy farmer claiming she was attacked but couldn't remember what happened or even HER own name. After Alex's body was found her story quickly unraveled, revealing her twisted game.

media:content.media:title: Ezra McCandless: The Revenge of Killer Ex-Girlfriend
media:content.media:description:
On the afternoon of March 22, 2018, Ezra McCandless stabbed 24-year-old philosophy student Alex Woodworth 16 times in a dairy farm near Eau Claire, Wisconsin.
Four hours after he was stabbed, Ezra knocked on the door of a dairy farmer claiming she was attacked but couldn't remember what happened or even HER own name. After Alex's body was found her story quickly unraveled, revealing her twisted game.

item.title: Susan Smith: Someone Stole My Babies!
item.description: On October 25th, 1994, Susan Smith looked at her sons, Michael and Alex strapped in the car but before releasing the parking brake she left the car and watched her children drown in a lake. Susan Smith claimed it was a black man who kidnapped her children at gunpoint which would soon turn out to be a lie. For nine days, she made dramatic pleas on national television for their safe return. However, following an intensive investigation and a nationwide search for them, she confessed on November 3rd, 1994, to letting her car roll into the lake drowning them inside.

media:content.media:title: Susan Smith: Someone Stole My Babies!
media:content.media:description: On October 25th, 1994, Susan Smith looked at her sons, Michael and Alex strapped in the car but before releasing the parking brake she left the car and watched her children drown in a lake. Susan Smith claimed it was a black man who kidnapped her children at gunpoint which would soon turn out to be a lie. For nine days, she made dramatic pleas on national television for their safe return. However, following an intensive investigation and a nationwide search for them, she confessed on November 3rd, 1994, to letting her car roll into the lake drowning them inside.

item.title: Shauna Howe's Kidnapping Made an Entire Town Cancel Halloween For 15 Years
item.description:
In October 1992, Oil City, Pennsylvania was preparing for Halloween like it always had. Decorations were up, kids were attending parties, and parents still believed their town was safe enough for children to walk home alone. Eleven year old Girl Scout Shauna Howe left a church Halloween party just after 8 p.m., only a few blocks from her house.
She never made it home. A witness watched as a man grabbed Shauna off the street, covered her mouth, and rushed her away as a red car sped off nearby. Despite a fast police response, Shauna was gone. Two days later, her costume was found miles away. Her body was discovered beneath an abandoned railroad trestle, showing signs of extreme violence.
For years, the case stalled as technology lagged behind the evidence. Oil City cancelled Halloween, replacing it with vigils and silence. Nearly a decade later, inmate testimony and DNA finally revealed the truth. Shauna had been kidnapped and murdered by James and Timothy O’Brien, with the help of Eldred “Ted” Walker. All three were convicted. The town eventually brought Halloween back, but the fear and loss never fully left.

media:content.media:title: Shauna Howe's Kidnapping Made an Entire Town Cancel Halloween For 15 Years
media:content.media:description:
In October 1992, Oil City, Pennsylvania was preparing for Halloween like it always had. Decorations were up, kids were attending parties, and parents still believed their town was safe enough for children to walk home alone. Eleven year old Girl Scout Shauna Howe left a church Halloween party just after 8 p.m., only a few blocks from her house.
She never made it home. A witness watched as a man grabbed Shauna off the street, covered her mouth, and rushed her away as a red car sped off nearby. Despite a fast police response, Shauna was gone. Two days later, her costume was found miles away. Her body was discovered beneath an abandoned railroad trestle, showing signs of extreme violence.
For years, the case stalled as technology lagged behind the evidence. Oil City cancelled Halloween, replacing it with vigils and silence. Nearly a decade later, inmate testimony and DNA finally revealed the truth. Shauna had been kidnapped and murdered by James and Timothy O’Brien, with the help of Eldred “Ted” Walker. All three were convicted. The town eventually brought Halloween back, but the fear and loss never fully left.

item.title: Four Cult Leaders You May Not Know About
item.description: Today on the podcast, we discuss four cult leaders you may not know about. First, Amy discusses cult leader Anna Young and the House of Prayer, and the tragic abuse that occurred there. Next, Chris explains how cult leader Lawrence Ray manipulated his way into his daughter's dorm and recruited her roommates to form a cult. Next up, Amy describes the mysterious death of businessman turned cult leader Mark Foster. Finally, Chris discusses the cult leader Amy Carlson and the cult Love Has Won. Carlson convinced her followers that she had been reincarnated 534 times, including as Jesus, Cleopatra, Joan of Arc, Queen Elizabeth, and Marilyn Monroe.

media:content.media:title: Four Cult Leaders You May Not Know About
media:content.media:description: Today on the podcast, we discuss four cult leaders you may not know about. First, Amy discusses cult leader Anna Young and the House of Prayer, and the tragic abuse that occurred there. Next, Chris explains how cult leader Lawrence Ray manipulated his way into his daughter's dorm and recruited her roommates to form a cult. Next up, Amy describes the mysterious death of businessman turned cult leader Mark Foster. Finally, Chris discusses the cult leader Amy Carlson and the cult Love Has Won. Carlson convinced her followers that she had been reincarnated 534 times, including as Jesus, Cleopatra, Joan of Arc, Queen Elizabeth, and Marilyn Monroe.
